project_name: Value-per-Joule Broker Online Canary on GB10 Endpoints

decision.recommended_next_action: Finalize this branch as a supported controlled online canary and use the produced harness if a separate longer production-soak project is later desired.
decision.stop_reason: A branch-local online canary harness was implemented, regression-tested, and run against temporary real GB10 llama.cpp endpoints with all rollback guards passing.

recommended_next_action: Finalize this branch as a supported controlled online canary and use the produced harness if a separate longer production-soak project is later desired.
run_notes_tail: - verifies `/models` and `/chat/completions` on OpenAI-compatible endpoint routes,
- shadows each request across `small_model`, `retrieval_tool`, and `frontier_model`,
- routes the canary arm through the measured-calibrated value-per-joule policy,
- compares against confidence-only and static tiering baselines,
- writes JSON/CSV/Markdown artifacts,
- evaluates rollback guards.
- Added `tests/test_online_canary.py` for guard behavior and replay-mode canary validation.
### Branch-specific kill condition

Kill/rollback this branch if any controlled canary run has: value-per-joule cost-per-success reduction <20% vs confidence-only, joules-per-success regression, quality drop >5 pp, SLA hit-rate drop >0.25 pp, p95 latency increase >100 ms, endpoint error rate >2%, or fewer than 12 observed requests.
### Endpoint discovery
- Wrote `results/endpoint_discovery.json` after checking common local ports.
- Existing GB10 dashboard ports (`11000`, `8080`) returned HTML, not OpenAI-compatible `/v1/models` JSON; `8001` and `8002` were initially closed.
### Validation runs
- Created a project-local `.venv` with `uv venv --python /usr/bin/python3 .venv` and installed `pytest` with `uv pip install --python .venv/bin/python pytest`.
- Regression tests: `.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q` => `15 passed in 0.11s`.
- Replay canary from measured parent telemetry: `results/online_canary_replay.json`, `results/online_canary_decisions.csv`, `results/online_canary_summary.md`.
- VPJ vs confidence-only: cost/success reduction 32.06%, joules/success reduction 27.92%, success delta 0.00 pp, SLA delta 0.00 pp, p95 latency delta -71.09 ms.
- All rollback guards passed.

- Live local GB10 canary: launched temporary llama.cpp servers from the project command with logs in `results/live_small_llama_server.log` and `results/live_frontier_llama_server.log`; the command trap stopped both servers before yielding.
- Small route: SmolLM2-135M GGUF on `127.0.0.1:8001`.
- Frontier route: Qwen3-0.6B GGUF on `127.0.0.1:8002`.
- Artifacts: `results/online_canary_live.json`, `results/online_canary_live_decisions.csv`, `results/online_canary_live_summary.md`.
- VPJ vs confidence-only: cost/success reduction 79.44%, joules/success reduction 82.52%, success delta +5.56 pp, SLA delta 0.00 pp, p95 latency delta -167.17 ms.
- All rollback guards passed; no helper servers remained listening on ports 8001/8002 afterward.
## Conclusion

The branch successfully moved the parent offline value-per-joule broker into a controlled online canary harness and validated it against real local GB10 llama.cpp endpoints plus replayed measured telemetry. The hypothesis is supported for this controlled canary scope.
- verifies `/models` and `/chat/completions` on OpenAI-compatible endpoint routes,
- shadows each request across `small_model`, `retrieval_tool`, and `frontier_model`,
- routes the canary arm through the measured-calibrated value-per-joule policy,
- compares against confidence-only and static tiering baselines,
- writes JSON/CSV/Markdown artifacts,
- evaluates rollback guards.
- Added `tests/test_online_canary.py` for guard behavior and replay-mode canary validation.

- Small route: SmolLM2-135M GGUF on `127.0.0.1:8001`.
- Frontier route: Qwen3-0.6B GGUF on `127.0.0.1:8002`.

- VPJ vs confidence-only: cost/success reduction 79.44%, joules/success reduction 82.52%, success delta +5.56 pp, SLA delta 0.00 pp, p95 latency delta -167.17 ms.
